title: Executive Powers (Agricultural Economics and Marketing) Transfer Proclamation, 1977
title_en: Executive Powers (Agricultural Economics and Marketing) Transfer Proclamation, 1977

provisions.title: Definition
provisions.content: (1) In this Proclamation “General Proclamation” means the Executive Powers Transfer (General Provisions) Proclamation, 1977 (Proclamation No. AG. 7 of 1977). (2) A reference in this Proclamation to any particular law, shall be construed as including a reference to a regulation, rule or other enactment made under or relating to that law.

provisions.title: Transfer of administration of Department of Agricultural Economics and Marketing
provisions.content: Notwithstanding anything to the contrary contained in any other law but subject to the provisions of this Proclamation and the General Proclamation, the administration of the affairs of the territory in relation to any matter which at the commencement of this Proclamation is administered by the Minister of Agriculture in the Department of Agricultural Economics and Marketing of the Republic, shall be carried on by the Administrator-General.

provisions.title: Application of laws
provisions.content: (1) The provisions of subsection (1) of section 3 of the General Proclamation shall, without detracting from the provisions of subsection (2) of that section, not apply to - (a) the reference to the Republic in section 13 of the Dairy Industry Act, 1961 (Act 30 of 1961 ); (b) those provisions of the Marketing Act, 1968 (Act 59 of 1968 ), that relate to the Wool Scheme published under the Marketing Act, 1968, by Proclamation R. 155 of 1972. [paragraph (b) amended by AG 41 of 1978] (2) Notwithstanding the provisions of section 3(2)(b) of the General Proclamation, the reference to the Minister of Agriculture in sections 3(1)(c) and (e) and 46(j) and (l) of the Abattoir Industry Act, 1976 (Act 54 of 1976 ), and in those provisions that relate to the control of the powers, functions and duties of the National Marketing Council and the Agricultural Reference Board, shall be construed as a reference to the Administrator-General.

provisions.title: Short title
provisions.content: This Proclamation shall be called the Executive Powers (Agricultural Economics and Marketing) Transfer Proclamation, 1977. History of this document 01 July 1978 this version Amended by Karakul Scheme Amendment Proclamation, 1978 20 December 1977 Published in Official Gazette 3681 Commenced 07 December 1977 Assented to Related documents [] [] [] [] ▲ To the top > Contact us Visit our Facebook Page Visit our Twitter/X profile About NamibLII NamibLII is a project of the Law Reform and Development Commission managed by the Directorate: Law Reform of the Ministry of Justice of the Republic of Namibia. In terms of the Law Reform and Development Commission Act, 1991 (Act No. 29 of 1991), the Commission is mandated to consolidate or codify any branch of the law or to introduce other measures aimed at making the law more readily accessible. NamibLII is the embodiment of this mandate making annotated statutes, judgments, and gazettes available in a user-friendly format.
